description: Add missing plugin_list_tabs browser tool to documentation and update stale '39 built-in browser tools' count to 40 across all pages that reference it.
qualityChecks: cd docs && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run knip && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Add plugin_list_tabs to browser tools reference page
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want plugin_list_tabs documented in the browser tools reference so that I can discover how to list matching tabs and their readiness status before targeting specific tabs.
acceptanceCriteria: plugin_list_tabs has a row in the browser-tools.mdx 'Site Analysis' section (or a new 'Plugin Discovery' section) with description: 'List open browser tabs that match a plugin's URL patterns, with per-tab readiness status. Returns tab IDs, URLs, titles, and readiness for each matching tab. Use this to discover available tabs before targeting a specific one with the tabId parameter on plugin tools. When called without a plugin argument, returns tabs for all plugins.'
The summary table at the bottom of browser-tools.mdx shows the updated category count (Site Analysis becomes 2, or Plugin Discovery is added with count 1) and the total row reads 40 instead of 39
The frontmatter description in browser-tools.mdx reads '40 built-in browser tools' instead of '39'
The lastUpdated frontmatter field in browser-tools.mdx is set to 2026-02-28
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
File: docs/content/docs/reference/browser-tools.mdx

The tool plugin_list_tabs is implemented in platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/plugin-list-tabs.ts. It is classified as 'observe' security tier (auto-allowed). Its input schema has one optional parameter: plugin (string, optional — when provided returns tabs for that plugin only, when omitted returns tabs for all plugins). It reads directly from server-side state (no extension dispatch). It uses the 'plugin_' prefix, same as plugin_analyze_site.

The current 'Site Analysis' category in the summary table has count 1 (plugin_analyze_site). The simplest fix is to rename that category to 'Plugin Tools' (or similar) with count 2 and add plugin_list_tabs as a new row in the same table section as plugin_analyze_site. Alternatively, add a new section. The summary table total on line 362 must change from 39 to 40.

Also update the frontmatter description on line 3 from '39 built-in browser tools' to '40 built-in browser tools'.

userStories.title: Update '39 built-in browser tools' references to 40 across docs
userStories.description: As a user reading the docs, I want the browser tool count to be accurate so that I'm not misled about the platform's capabilities.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/content/docs/index.mdx references '40 built-in browser tools' instead of '39 built-in browser tools'
docs/content/docs/quick-start.mdx references '40 built-in browser tools' instead of '39 built-in browser tools'
docs/content/docs/install/index.mdx references '40 built-in browser tools' instead of '39 built-in browser tools'
The lastUpdated frontmatter field is updated to 2026-02-28 in each of the three files modified
No other occurrences of '39 built-in' or '39 browser' remain in any .mdx file under docs/content/docs/
cd docs && npm run build passes
cd docs && npm run type-check passes
cd docs && npm run lint passes
cd docs && npm run knip passes
cd docs && npm run format:check passes
userStories.notes:
Three files need a simple text replacement of '39' to '40' in their browser tool count references:

1. docs/content/docs/index.mdx line 33: '39 built-in browser tools' → '40 built-in browser tools'
2. docs/content/docs/quick-start.mdx line 74: 'plus 39 built-in browser tools' → 'plus 40 built-in browser tools'
3. docs/content/docs/install/index.mdx line 75: 'the 39 built-in browser tools' → 'the 40 built-in browser tools'

Each file also needs its lastUpdated frontmatter field updated to 2026-02-28. The frontmatter is at the top of each file in YAML format between --- delimiters.
